question_text: A rectangular block of metal has a mass of 240 g. When placed in a measuring cylinder containing 50 cm³ of water, the water level rises to 80 cm³. Calculate the density of the metal in g/cm³. Will this metal float or sink in water? Explain your answer.
correct_answer: Volume of metal = 80 - 50 = 30 cm³. Density = mass ÷ volume = 240 ÷ 30 = 8 g/cm³. The metal will sink because its density (8 g/cm³) is greater than the density of water (1.0 g/cm³).
explanation: First, find the volume using displacement: the difference between final and initial water levels. Then use ρ = m/V to calculate density. Since 8 g/cm³ > 1.0 g/cm³, the metal is denser than water and will sink.
